chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS C.P No.D-1789 of 2024. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 03.09.2025. Mr. Velji Rathore, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Sharif Solangi, Assistant A.G Sindh a/w Mr. Nor Din Memon, Regional Director L.G Mirpurkhas on behalf of Secretary, L.G Sindh Karachi (ii) Allah Bachayo, Additional Director L.G Tharparkar (iii) Sawai Singh, Chairman U.C Jhirmirio (iv) Muhammad Yousuf Secretary U.C Jhirmirio. Mir Sarfaraz Ali Talpur, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of Respondent No.6, which is taken on record. The Respondent No.1, 3 & 6 have submitted their reports/statements/para wise comments, which are taken on record and copies thereof have also been supplied to the counsel for Petitioner and learned A.A.G Sindh. Through this Petition, the Petitioner mainly prays regarding stoppage of his salary since December, 2023. The counsel for respondent No.6 and officials / Respondents present in Court submit that the Petitioner was issued three show-cause notices as he remained absent from his duties and only one show-cause notice was replied. Mr. A.A.G further brought attention of this Court that the Petitioner was relieved and his services were terminated by Chairman U.C Jhirmirio Taluka Diplo vide letter No.UC/JHM/66/of 2024 dated 08.07.2024 after issuing three show-cause notices as prescribed under the Rules. In view of above circumstances, it seems that now the services of Petitioners have terminated and since the Petition in hand is only regarding stoppage of salary, which apparently has become infructuous, hence the same is hereby disposed of being infructuous.

A.A.G further brought attention of this Court that the Petitioner was relieved and his services were terminated by Chairman U.C Jhirmirio Taluka Diplo vide letter No.UC/JHM/66/of 2024 dated 08.07.2024 after issuing three show-cause notices as prescribed under the Rules. In view of above circumstances, it seems that now the services of Petitioners have terminated and since the Petition in hand is only regarding stoppage of salary, which apparently has become infructuous, hence the same is hereby disposed of being infructuous. However, the Petitioner is at liberty to challenge his termination letter at proper forum or may file another Petition subject to maintainability if he desires so. JUDGE JUDGE Ali.
